title: PhD
biography: Edward Lewis was an American geneticist who discovered how genes control the body plan of developing organisms. His work on homeotic genes revolutionized developmental biology. Nobel Prize 1995.
aiSummary: Edward Lewis is a genetics specialist at California Institute of Technology. Based in Pasadena.

affiliations.role: Thomas Hunt Morgan Professor of Biology

awards: Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine (1995)

timeline.title: Mapped homeotic genes
timeline.description: Showed how clusters of genes control body segment identity in Drosophila

timeline.title: Nobel Prize
timeline.description: For discoveries concerning the genetic control of early embryonic development
